svg.text: Mindfulness while Breathing In and Out
Mindfulness while breathing in and out, when cultivated and frequently practiced, is of great fruit and great benefit
ānāpānassati bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā
CULTIVATION OF MINDFULNESS WHILE BREATHING IN AND OUT
Ever mindful, he breathes in; ever mindful, he breathes out
I. OBSERVING THE BODY IN AND OF ITSELF
Breathing in long, breathing out long
dīghaṁ vā assasanto, dīghaṁ vā passasanto
Breathing in short, breathing out short
rassaṁ vā assasanto, rassaṁ vā passasanto
II. OBSERVING FELT EXPERIENCE IN AND OF ITSELF
III. OBSERVING THE MIND IN AND OF ITSELF
IV. OBSERVING MENTAL QUALITIES IN AND OF THEMSELVES
The four establishments of mindfulness fulfill the seven factors of awakening
evaṁ bhāvitā cattāro satipaṭṭhānā satta sambojjhaṅge paripūrenti
Dwelling thus mindful, he investigates with wisdom
Awakening Factor of Investigation of States
Investigates and examines that state with wisdom
Investigating with wisdom, unflagging energy is aroused
In one who has aroused energy, joy arises
Joy free from worldly ties arises
In one uplifted with joy, body and mind become tranquil
Body becomes tranquil and mind becomes tranquil
In one whose body is tranquil, the mind becomes collected
Having collected the mind, he observes with equanimity
FOUR SUPPORTS FOR THE CULTIVATION OF AWAKENING FACTORS · catu nissaya
The seven factors of awakening, when cultivated and frequently practiced, fulfill true knowledge and liberation
evaṁ bhāvitā satta bojjhaṅgā evaṁ bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrenti
